commentary: so the last part here is about dukkha or suffering to carbon can be translated as suffering or sorrow or unsatisfactoriness etc now here dukkha is generally defined as being born as dukkha aging is too kasich dying is dukkha sorrow lamentation pain grief despair dukkha not to get what 1 wants is to cut in short the 5 aggregates of clinging is dukkha the 5 aggregate of clinging is basically body and mind the mind part consists of 4 things feelings perception volition and consciousness so these 4 things plus body makes up the 5 aggregates of clinging these are the 5 things that we all cling to and which we all consider to be the self whenever you refer to yourself in the past life or in the future life or in the present life you always refer to yourself in connection with these 5 things these 5 aggregates body feelings perception volition consciousness these 5 things and it is because of clinging to these 5 things that suffering arises now there are some ducat that is above measure that is very extreme very great then some ducati is trifling some dukkha that is quick to change and some dukkha that is slow to change and then when 1 is overcome by dukkha 1 grieves mourns laments beats his breast and becomes wielded or roams about in search of 1 who knows a spell or 2 to end the dukkah dukkha yields bewilderment and search i say now this dukkha yields bewilderment and search it is because of this result of dukkha that dukkha is a very good teacher if not for dukkha we would not want to walk this spiritual path it is because as we go through life and we suffer then we get bewildered and then we search for a way to end our dukkha and if we are sincere enough we will come across the right dhamma and that will lead us out of suffering is the best teacher in the world if you if a person doesn't has not had enough of dukkha then if you try to teach him the spiritual path he would not be interested it is only at a certain time when a person is spiritually mature through suffering then that person is interested to cultivate the holy life to to endure [Music]
